revision.text:
[[ja:大分八幡宮]] {{afc comment |1= taking a source from [[Daibu temple ruins]] did not check it myself as it was checked by the Wikipedia community [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 18 March 2025 (UTC)|qq=}} {{infobox religious building |名称 = {{ill|Daibu Hachiman-gu|ja|大分八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibu Hachiman-gu|qq=}} |image = File:Daibu.jpg |所在地 = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Iizuka, Fukuoka|en|Iizuka, Fukuoka|qid=Q328605|lt=Iizuka City|1=Iizuka, Fukuoka}} Daibu 1272 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 04 |経度度 = 130|経度分 = 37|経度秒 = 32 |祭神 = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56344982}} |社格 = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |創建 = {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|qid=Q1373210|lt=Shinkame|1=Jinki (era)}} 3rd year (726) |本殿 = Sanma-sha ryū-zukuri (three-bay shrine style) |別名 = {{ill|Oita Miyu|ja|大分宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita Miyu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大分八幡宮|1=Daibu Hachiman-gu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11432652}} |例祭 = September 30 – October 1 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|qq=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Iizuka|qid=Q328605|1=Iizuka, Fukuoka}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.584444|N|130.625556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Daibu Hachiman-gu''' (pronounced "Daibu Hachamangū") is a Shinto shrine located in [[Iizuka City]] of {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. Its former shrine Ranking was that of a Gōsha. It is known as the original shrine (moto-miya) of {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|en|Hakozaki Shrine|qid=Q714742|lt=Hakozaki Shrine|1=Hakozaki Shrine}} {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}, and is one of the five Hachiman shrines. It is also the origin shrine of [[:en:Usa Hachimangu|Usa Hachimangu]] and thus of most Hachiman shrines. == Five Hachiman Shrines == In Kyushu, there are five famous Hachiman shrines:https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=27c5670aeede03b2de338801bf5ee879d8675bcad08049a60d67c15e39806567JmltdHM9MTc0MDcwMDgwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=03ed158f-b127-659c-17ab-002eb05f646b&psq=%e4%ba%94%e6%89%80%e5%85%ab%e5%b9%a1&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9rb3RvYmFuay5qcC93b3JkLyVFNCVCQSU5NCVFNiU4OSU4MCVFNSU4NSVBQiVFNSVCOSVBMS01MDE1NDI&ntb=1 * Chikuzen’s {{ill|Daibu Hachiman-gu|ja|大分八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibu Hachiman-gū|qq=}} ([[:ja:大分宮|大分宮]]) * Hizen’s [[Chiriku Hachimangu]] ([[:ja:千栗ちりく宮|千栗ちりく宮]]) * Higo’s [[Fujisaki Hachimangū]] ([[:ja:藤崎宮|藤崎宮]]) * Satsuma’s Shinden Shrine ([[:ja:新田宮|新田宮]]) * Osumi’s Seihachiman ([[:ja:正八幡|正八幡]]) Today, there are others of a similar kind as well. These are known as the "Five Great Hachiman Shrines" (五大八幡宮). == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56344982}} == Origin == Daibu Hachiman-gu was founded in the 3rd year of {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|qid=Q1373210|lt=Shinkame|1=Jinki (era)}} (726) during the Nara period. According to shrine tradition, the location of this shrine is the place where {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} temporarily stayed on her return journey from the conquest of the Three Kingdoms. == Facilities == * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) : The current shrine buildings – both the Haiden and Honden – were extensively renovated in Heisei 7 (1995). * Shimon (Divine Gate) * Hojo Pond – a pond used during the Hojo Festival. * The Well of Emperor Ōjin’s Birth Water * Great Camphor Tree – an estimated 350-year-old camphor tree (【クスノキ】) designated as a Natural Monument of Fukuoka Prefecture. According to shrine legend, it is said to be a descendant of the tree that {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} brought back from the Korean Peninsula. * Uguisuzuka – a hill about 10 m tall located in a detached part of the shrine grounds. Tradition holds that Empress Jingū disbanded her troops here, and it is said that the name “Oita” derives from this site. == Annual Festival == At the annual festival (Hojo-kai) held every September, the “Daibu Lion Dance” is performed. This lion dance, designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture, is said to have been transmitted from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qid=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} in the Edo period (specifically in Kyōhō 9, 1724) and—along with the earth-worker lion dance of Kagawachō in Kaho District—has influenced lion dances in southern Fukuoka. It is also noted that the festival chants and shouts bear a strong resemblance to Kyoto dialect. == Access == * From {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} on the {{ill|Fukuhoku Yutaka Line|en|Fukuhoku Yutaka Line|qid=Q5507634|lt=Fukuhoku Yutaka Line|1=Fukuhoku Yutaka Line}} at [[Chikuzen Oita Station]], it is approximately a 15-minute walk. == See Also == * [[Daibu temple ruins]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.daibu-hachiman.com/ 【Official】 Daibu Hachiman-gu] * [http://www.kankou-iizuka.jp/homepage/sub.asp?id=90&no=1 Daibu Hachiman-gu (Iizuka City website)] {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Normdaten|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|大分八幡宮|version=94840391|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Daibu Hachiman-gū}} [[ja:大分八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11432652}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iizuka, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Iizuka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:福岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:飯塚市の建築物]] [[Category:飯塚市の歴史]]
